problem: Is‚ąĎ (p_n)/(2^n)irrational? (Here p_n is the nth prime.)
additional_context:
ErdŇĎs \cite{Er58b} proved that ‚ąĎ (p_n^k)/(n!) is irrational for every k‚Č• 1.

In \cite{Er88c} he further conjectures that ‚ąĎ (p_n^k)/(2^n) is irrational for every k, and that if g_n‚Č• 2 and g_n=o(p_n) then‚ąĎ_{n=1}^‚ąě (p_n)/(g_1\cdots g_n)is irrational. (The example g_n=p_n+1 shows that some condition on the growth of the g_n is necessary here.)

The decimal expansion of this sum is A098990 on the OEIS.

References

[Er58b] ErdŇĎs, Paul, Sur certaines s\'{e}ries \`a{} valeur irrationnelle. Enseign. Math. (2) (1958), 93--100.

[Er88c] Erd\"{o}s, P., On the irrationality of certain series: problems and results. New advances in transcendence theory (Durham, 1986) (1988), 102-109.
reference_proof_hint:
Let

[
S=\sum_{n\ge1}\frac{p_n}{2^n}\qquad(p_n=\text{the }n\text{th prime}).
]

As of today (January 2026), **it is not known** whether $S$ is rational or irrational.

This question was raised by ErdŇĎs, and he **conjectured** that this kind of series should be irrational [[nomath]](more generally $\sum p_n^k/2^n$ for each fixed $k\ge1$)[[/nomath]], but he did not have a proof even for (k=1). 

It is also listed as an **open ErdŇĎs problem** (ErdŇĎs Problem #251). ([erdosproblems.com][1])

If you want to see the numerical value, its decimal expansion is recorded in OEIS as **A098990**. ([oeis.org][2])

[1]: https://www.erdosproblems.com/251 "
  
    ErdŇĎs Problem #251
  
"
[2]: https://oeis.org/A098990 "A098990 - OEIS"

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 251

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/251](https://www.erdosproblems.com/251)
-/

namespace Erdos251

/--
Is $\sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{p_n}{2^n}$ irrational? Here $p_n$ is the $n$-th prime ($p_1=2, p_2=3, \dots$).
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_251 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ Irrational (‚ąĎ' n : ‚Ąē, (Nat.nth Nat.Prime n) / (2 ^ n)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos251
